basic_info.name: ElizaOS (ai16z)
basic_info.status: Production (v1 stable and widely deployed; v2 released January 2025 with major architecture overhaul; largest open-source crypto-AI agent framework by GitHub stars and community size)
basic_info.ecosystem: Multi-chain (Solana primary via ai16z DAO; Ethereum/EVM support; Base; supports multiple chains through plugin architecture; ELIZA token on Solana)
basic_info.launch_date: October 2024 (initial release as 'ai16z/eliza' by Shaw/ai16z DAO; renamed to ElizaOS; v2 released January 2025 with unified message bus, agent wallet, and registry)

onboarding_flow.registration_steps: 1) Install ElizaOS: Clone the repository and install dependencies (Node.js/TypeScript project, uses pnpm). Run 'pnpm install' and 'pnpm build'. 2) Create a Character Configuration: Define the agent's personality, bio, lore, message examples, style guidelines, and plugin list in a JSON character file. This character config is the agent's persistent identity definition. 3) Configure Environment: Set up environment variables for LLM API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, local models, etc.), database connections (SQLite default, PostgreSQL optional), and any platform-specific credentials (Discord bot token, Twitter API keys, Telegram bot token, etc.). 4) Select and Configure Plugins: Choose from available plugins for capabilities: blockchain wallets (Solana, EVM), social media connectors (Discord, Twitter, Telegram, Farcaster), DeFi protocols, knowledge retrieval, image generation, etc. V2 uses a plugin registry for discovery. 5) Start the Agent: Run 'pnpm start --characters characters/your-agent.json'. The agent starts, loads its character config, initializes plugins, connects to configured platforms, and begins operating. 6) For V2 Agent Registry: Register the agent in the ElizaOS registry to make it discoverable by other agents. This involves publishing agent metadata and capabilities. 7) For Agent Wallet (V2): Initialize the agent's built-in wallet for autonomous on-chain transactions. The wallet supports Solana and EVM chains. 8) Connect to Message Bus (V2): The unified message bus enables agent-to-agent communication within the ElizaOS ecosystem.
onboarding_flow.human_involvement: full-setup (human must write character configuration, set up environment variables with API keys, choose plugins, and deploy the agent; ongoing maintenance for plugin updates and character tuning; V2 agent wallet may require initial funding by human)
onboarding_flow.agent_autonomy: After initial human setup, agents operate autonomously: responding to messages across platforms (Discord, Twitter, Telegram), executing on-chain transactions via agent wallet, interacting with other agents via message bus (V2), managing their own memory and conversation context. Agents cannot self-register or self-configure; they require human-authored character files and environment setup. V2 adds more autonomous capabilities through the agent wallet (self-custody, autonomous trading) and registry (self-discovery).
onboarding_flow.time_to_first_action: minutes (installation and basic setup can be completed in under 30 minutes; first message response occurs immediately upon startup; on-chain actions require wallet funding which adds time; plugin-dependent features may require additional API key provisioning)

identity_model.identity_type: Character config (JSON file defining personality, bio, knowledge, behavior rules) + optional wallet (Solana/EVM wallet address for on-chain identity). V2 adds agent registry entries. Identity is primarily off-chain via the character config, with optional on-chain presence through wallet addresses.
identity_model.key_management: LLM API keys and platform credentials stored in .env files or environment variables (no built-in secret management). Agent wallet keys (V2): private keys generated locally; for Solana, standard keypair generation; for EVM, standard private key/mnemonic. Keys are stored in the agent's local database or environment. No built-in HSM/KMS integration. V2 TEE (Trusted Execution Environment) plugin provides hardware-secured key storage for agent wallets in supported environments. Key rotation: not natively supported for agent identity; changing keys requires manual reconfiguration. Platform API keys (Discord, Twitter) are managed by the human operator.
identity_model.multi_instance_support: Partial. The same character config can be loaded by multiple agent instances, creating multiple copies with the same personality but separate memory/state. V2's message bus can coordinate between instances. However, there is no native multi-instance identity management; each instance maintains its own conversation memory and state. Multiple instances sharing a wallet would create conflicts. [uncertain]
identity_model.recovery_mechanism: Character config backup: since identity is defined in a JSON file, backing up the character file preserves the agent's personality. Database backup: agent memory (conversation history, relationships, knowledge) is stored in SQLite or PostgreSQL and can be backed up/restored. Wallet recovery: standard mnemonic/seed phrase recovery for blockchain wallets. No built-in social recovery or automated backup. If the character file and database are lost, the agent's personality and memory are lost. [uncertain]
identity_model.versioning_support: Character configs can be versioned via standard version control (git). V2 plugin registry may include version metadata for plugins. No on-chain version tracking or hash appending for agent code/model versions. The framework itself is versioned (v1, v2) with distinct architectures. [uncertain]

security.authentication_method: API key (LLM providers, platform APIs); wallet signature (for on-chain transactions); platform-native authentication (Discord OAuth, Twitter API bearer tokens, Telegram bot tokens). No protocol-level agent-to-agent authentication in v1. V2 message bus may include authentication for inter-agent communication. [uncertain]
security.known_vulnerabilities: 1) API key exposure: .env files containing LLM API keys, platform tokens, and wallet private keys are at risk if the deployment environment is compromised; several community incidents of leaked API keys from public repositories. 2) Plugin supply chain: community-contributed plugins may contain malicious code; no formal security review process for plugins. 3) Prompt injection: agents responding to user messages on public platforms (Discord, Twitter) are vulnerable to prompt injection attacks that could make them reveal system prompts, execute unintended actions, or generate harmful content. 4) Wallet security: agent wallets holding crypto assets are targets; private keys stored in environment variables or local databases lack enterprise-grade protection. 5) Memory poisoning: persistent memory systems can be poisoned by adversarial inputs, causing long-term behavioral drift. 6) No formal security audit of the core framework published. [uncertain]
security.revocation_speed: seconds to minutes (platform bot tokens can be revoked instantly via platform settings; API keys can be rotated at the provider; agent can be shut down immediately by stopping the process; on-chain wallet compromise requires immediate fund transfer as there is no freeze mechanism for standard wallets) [uncertain]
security.anti_sybil: No built-in anti-Sybil mechanism. Any human can deploy unlimited agent instances. The ELIZA token launchpad has token creation fees as an economic barrier for token-associated agents. V2 agent registry may implement reputation or staking requirements but this is not documented. [uncertain]
security.data_exposure_risk: Environment variables expose: LLM API keys, platform OAuth tokens, wallet private keys, database credentials. Agent memory database contains: full conversation histories, user information, relationship data. Character configs expose: agent personality, system prompts, behavioral rules. On-chain wallet transactions are publicly visible. Social media interactions are public. V2 registry entries expose agent capabilities and metadata.
security.bot_to_bot_attack_surface: V1: No native agent-to-agent communication, so attack surface is limited to platform-mediated interactions (e.g., two agents talking in a Discord channel, susceptible to prompt injection via public messages). V2: The unified message bus introduces direct agent-to-agent communication, creating new attack vectors including malicious message injection, agent impersonation on the message bus, and coordinated manipulation. No built-in message validation, rate limiting, or content filtering for inter-agent messages. Agents using LLMs for message processing are inherently vulnerable to adversarial inputs. [uncertain]
security.skill_plugin_vetting: No formal vetting in v1. Plugins are community-contributed and loaded at runtime based on character config. V2 introduces a plugin registry that could enable vetting but no mandatory review process is documented. Plugins run in the same process as the agent with full system access; no sandboxing. The npm/pnpm package ecosystem provides basic supply chain protections (package signing, audit) but no ElizaOS-specific security review. [uncertain]
security.attestation_mechanism: V2 TEE plugin: provides hardware-based attestation for agents running in Trusted Execution Environments (Intel SGX/TDX), proving the agent is running expected code in a secure enclave. This enables verifiable agent integrity for high-security deployments. Without TEE, no attestation mechanism exists. Wallet signatures can prove control of on-chain identity but do not attest to agent code integrity. [uncertain]

offchain_onchain_linking.linking_support: yes (native hybrid architecture: off-chain character/personality with on-chain wallet identity and token)
offchain_onchain_linking.linking_mechanism: Agent wallet: the agent's blockchain wallet address serves as its on-chain identity, linked to its off-chain character config through the ElizaOS runtime. The agent signs on-chain transactions proving it controls the wallet. For ELIZA token launchpad agents: the token contract address links to the agent's off-chain personality and capabilities. V2 agent registry may provide structured linking between off-chain agent metadata and on-chain identities. No formal EIP-712 binding or VC-based linking; the linking is implicit through the runtime configuration.
offchain_onchain_linking.transfer_behavior: Wallet-based: transferring wallet control (sharing private key/mnemonic) transfers on-chain identity. Character configs are portable files that can be transferred independently. ELIZA tokens are freely tradeable ERC-20/SPL tokens whose transfer does not affect agent operation. No formal identity transfer mechanism; the human operator controls both the character config and wallet keys. [uncertain]

delegation_and_authorization.delegation_model: No formal delegation model. Human-to-agent delegation is implicit: the human writes the character config and provides API keys/wallet access, effectively delegating authority to the agent within those bounds. Agent-to-agent delegation in V2 can occur through the message bus (one agent requesting another to perform a task) but there is no formal authorization framework. Direct key sharing is the practical delegation mechanism for wallet access. [uncertain]
delegation_and_authorization.authorization_granularity: Coarse (plugin-level). Authorization is determined by which plugins are loaded and which API keys/credentials are provided. If the Discord plugin is loaded with a bot token, the agent has full access to that bot's Discord permissions. If a wallet plugin is loaded with a private key, the agent has full control of that wallet. No fine-grained permission system, ABAC, or per-action authorization. [uncertain]

discovery_and_interoperability.discovery_mechanism: V2 Agent Registry: agents register in a centralized ElizaOS registry with metadata about their capabilities, personality, and endpoints. Other agents and users can search the registry to discover available agents. V1: no native discovery; agents are found through their social media presence (Discord servers, Twitter accounts) or direct configuration. ELIZA token launchpad provides discovery via the token marketplace.
discovery_and_interoperability.capability_declaration: Character config (JSON file declaring personality, supported platforms, loaded plugins, behavioral rules). V2 registry entries may include structured capability metadata. Plugin list serves as an implicit capability declaration. No formal Agent Card, JSON-LD, or standardized capability schema. [uncertain]
discovery_and_interoperability.cross_platform_portability: Partially portable. Character configs are portable JSON files that can run on any ElizaOS instance. Memory/state is stored in databases that can be migrated. However, agent identity is not portable to non-ElizaOS frameworks. Wallet identity (blockchain address) is portable across any blockchain-compatible system. No W3C DID or universal identity standard support.
discovery_and_interoperability.standards_compliance: No formal standard compliance. Uses TypeScript/Node.js ecosystem conventions. Blockchain interactions follow standard Solana/EVM protocols. Social media integrations use platform-specific APIs (Discord.js, Twitter API v2). No W3C DID, W3C VC, OIDC, SPIFFE, OAuth 2.0, ERC-721, or ERC-8004 compliance. V2 TEE plugin implements Intel SGX/TDX attestation standards. [uncertain]
discovery_and_interoperability.protocol_composability: Moderate. Plugin architecture enables composability with external systems: MCP plugin for Model Context Protocol integration, blockchain plugins for DeFi protocol interaction, social media plugins for platform integration. V2 message bus enables inter-agent composability within ElizaOS ecosystem. No native A2A, ANP, or ERC-8004 integration but these could be implemented as plugins. The plugin architecture is the primary composability mechanism. [uncertain]

push_pull_communication.push_support: Platform-native push: Discord (WebSocket via Discord.js), Telegram (webhook/long-polling), Twitter (streaming API). V2 message bus: provides pub/sub-style push messaging between agents. WebSocket for real-time agent communication in V2. [uncertain]
push_pull_communication.pull_support: REST API (ElizaOS exposes a local REST API for querying agent state and sending messages). Platform polling (Twitter mentions check, Discord message polling as fallback). V2 registry provides pull-based agent discovery. Direct HTTP API for programmatic agent interaction.
push_pull_communication.subscription_model: Platform-specific: Discord event subscriptions (message events, reaction events), Twitter stream subscriptions, Telegram update subscriptions. V2 message bus: channel-based subscriptions where agents subscribe to topics/channels for inter-agent communication. No blockchain event subscriptions natively but achievable through plugins. [uncertain]

trust_and_reputation.trust_model: Centralized (platform-verified) for social media identity (verified Discord bots, Twitter accounts). No decentralized trust model. V2 agent registry may provide platform-level trust signals. ELIZA token market cap and trading volume serve as market-based reputation proxy. No formal trust framework or credential verification. [uncertain]
trust_and_reputation.reputation_system: No formal reputation system. Market-based signals: ELIZA token price/volume, social media follower counts, community engagement metrics. V2 could introduce registry-based ratings but not documented. No on-chain reputation registry, VC-based reputation, or staking history tracking. Agent performance is judged by users through social media interactions. [uncertain]

payment_and_economics.payment_integration: Native token (ELIZA token on Solana for the ai16z ecosystem; agent tokens launched via ELIZA token launchpad); Solana SPL token transfers via wallet plugin; EVM token transfers via EVM wallet plugin; DeFi protocol integrations through plugins (Uniswap, Jupiter, Raydium, etc.); no AP2, ACP, or x402 integration documented. Agents can autonomously execute token swaps, provide liquidity, and manage portfolios through plugins.
payment_and_economics.economic_model: Free (open-source framework, no fees to deploy agents). Token launchpad has creation fees for launching agent tokens. Agent operation costs: LLM API usage fees (varies by provider), blockchain gas fees for on-chain transactions, hosting/compute costs. No staking requirement. ELIZA token serves as governance/utility token for the ai16z DAO ecosystem but is not required for agent deployment.

governance_and_compliance.regulatory_alignment: unknown - no explicit regulatory framework compliance. Open-source project under MIT license governed by ai16z DAO. No EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, or ISO 42001 compliance documented. The decentralized and open-source nature means compliance responsibility falls on individual deployers.
governance_and_compliance.audit_trail_capability: Local database logging: all conversations, actions, and state changes are stored in the agent's SQLite/PostgreSQL database, providing a per-agent audit trail. On-chain transaction history for wallet-based actions. No hash chain, signed event logs, or blockchain anchoring of agent decisions/conversations. Audit capability is basic and implementation-dependent. [uncertain]
governance_and_compliance.lifecycle_management: Registration (deploy agent with character config) / activation (start the agent process) / suspension (stop the process; can restart anytime) / upgrade (update character config or framework version; V2 plugin updates via registry) / decommissioning (stop process, optionally delete database). No formal migration mechanism between versions (v1 to v2 requires manual migration). No on-chain lifecycle tracking. [uncertain]
